We recommended that the (a) Municipal Treasurer evaluate the current use
of bank accounts and streamline the collection process by consolidating and/or closing
account/s, where appropriate, assuring such are utilized consistently for its intended
purpose; and (b) Municipal Accountant ensure strict compliance with Section 14 of
the MNGAS and the applicable provision of the GAM for LGUs by preparing
separate BRS for each bank account maintained by the Municipality, regardless of
transaction volume.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal
Accountant, at the beginning of each year, a certified list based on the updated
RPTAR or Taxpayer’s Index Card, detailing all amounts due and collectible from
real property taxpayers; (b) Municipal Accountant establish the RPT and SET
Receivables based on the duly certified list from the Treasury Office; and (c)
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant coordinate to establish a complete
list of delinquent RPT and SET taxpayers, and ensure that the same is fully reconciled
with the RPT and SET Receivable balances reported at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cleanse the dormant
account observing the guidelines and procedures in its proper disposition laid out in
COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that the (a) Inventory Committee comply with the physical
count of inventories, report it in RPCI and submit it not later than July 31 and
January 31 of each year; and (b) Municipal Accountant and GSO keep a record of
their inventories through SLCs and SCs for inventory management.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) derecognize the lots
exchanged or donated and recognize any resulting gain or loss from the same in
accordance with IPSAS 17; (ii) update the subsidiary ledgers and records to reflect
the return of control over the 400-sq.m lot under the exchange; and (iii) ensure proper
disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements (NFS) of the land transactions and
the resulting adjustments; and (b) GSO (i) strengthen property documentation and
records management by ensuring that all legal documents related to land assets, such
as Deeds of Donation/Exchange/Sale, Acceptance Documents, Tax Declarations and
TCTs are properly compiled, secured, and regularly monitored; and (ii) monitor and
immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant any property-related documents
that affect accounting records to ensure timely and accurate recognition or
derecognition.

We recommended that the GSO (a) ensure that all PARs are renewed every
three years or whenever there is a change in the end-user; (b) adhere with Local
Roads Asset Management System so that all vital data on roads and its road
components for every road network are reflected in pertinent reports and records;
and (c) register, renew and update the LTO registration of motor vehicles, as
warranted.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) recognize
depreciation starting on the following month after project completion; and (b) keep
a LC for each of the recorded CIP.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant recognize leave benefits
quarterly as they are earned, rather than recording the entire liability in a single year;
(b) Municipal Budget Officer establish an Employee Benefits Reserve Fund to fund
for all the leave benefits owed to employees; and (c) Municipal Human Resource
Management Office (MHRMO) coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office
and Municipal Budget Office to ensure adherence to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules
Implementing Book V of E.O. No. 292 on the yearly accounting and funding of leave
benefits.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant: (i) record the 20% share
from the BFP Fire Code Fees from the TF to the GF, consistent with the intent and
provisions of DOF-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2015-001 and ensure that the utilization of
the said fund undertake the regular appropriation process by obtaining approval
from the local Sanggunian; and (ii) facilitate the reconciliation of accounts with SAs
pertaining to said dormant balances and liquidate, if warranted, in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2016-005; otherwise, follow the procedures for the proper
disposition of dormant accounts in COA Circular No. 2023-008 and request the write-
off of these accounts; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman ensure
the preparation of project-specific Bidding Documents, particularly the Bid Data
Sheet and SCC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal Planning and
Development Office (MPDO) and other involved office/s to assure the (i) full delivery
of DF PPAs such that optimal use of the fund is achieved and that the LGU
constituents could immediately enjoy its intended benefits; and (ii) use of 20% DF in
keeping with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and (b) BAC to
submit the documentation deficiencies to the Audit Team.

We also recommended that, before entering into contract with the winning
bidder, the BAC ascertain that the CSHP is approved by the DOLE, as part of the
contract and agreement between the Municipality and the contractor.

We recommended that the (a) MDRRM Officer make certain that the
LDRRMFIP is formulated, reviewed, updated annually and approved by taking into
account all available funding sources for the execution of their DRRM-related PPAs
and such should be consistent with the format as demonstrated in COA Circular No.
2012-002; (b) Municipal Accountant adhere to the reporting guidelines of COA
Circular No. 2014-002 relative to the full disclosure in the NFS of DRRM information;
and (c) personnel concerned submit the documents found to be unattached to the DVs,
as noted.

We recommended that the GFPS (a) properly plan and implement programs
and activities under the GPB so that the identified gender issues could be fully
addressed; (b) expend the GAD budget within its limits; (c) formally adjust and
communicate all modifications to the originally endorsed GPB, and subsequently
provide COA with a copy of the revised GPB along with the acknowledgment receipt
from the concerned DILG office; (d) sex-disaggregate/quantify/specify the targets
and outcomes, as necessary; (e) apply the HGDG tool for all the GAD-attributed
programs and reflect them in the “attributed programs” section of the enhanced GPB
and GAD AR template; (f) constitute the GFPS and M&E Team in keeping with Items
4.1.A.2, 5.1 and 5.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and (g) submit
the GPB and GAD AR within the period set in COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor consider augmenting the
manpower complement of the BPLO; (b) Municipal Treasurer exercise its authority
to yearly examine the books of accounts and other pertinent records of businesses as
this can produce additional revenue to fund essential PPAs of the LGU; and (c) BPLO
ascertain that business permit applicants submit the applicable documentary
requirements and such are properly filed and maintained in their file folders.

We recommended that the LCE Implemented
direct the (i) BAC and Accounting
Office to submit the lacking
supporting documents, as
discussed, and ensure the
completeness of documentation
before the processing of payment
of claim/s; (5)
(ii) Engineering Office to enjoin Implemented
the infrastructure project
contractors to post the tarpaulin
signboards with sufficient details
with the necessary update thereof
of project status; (6) and
(iii) Municipal Accountant to use Implemented
the appropriate accounts in
recording and reporting the
involved financial transactions.
(7)

We recommended that the
LCE instruct the
(a) MDRRMC to (i) carefully
plan, implement, and prepare
the LDRRMFIP so that it
contains all required
information in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2012-
002 and list of projects to be
charged against the
unexpended fund of prior
years; and (4)
(ii) optimally utilize the
LDRRMF while charging only
those PPAs which are directly
related to disaster
preparedness, mitigation,
response, and rescue
operations so that the goal of
reducing disaster risk and
enhancing disaster
preparedness and response
capabilities will be achieved;
(5)
(b) Municipal Budget Officer
to maintain separate registries
for LDRRMF; and (6)
(c) Municipal Accountant to
(i) disclose in the Notes to the
Financial Statements complete
and necessary data for disaster
risk reduction and
management transactions; and
(ii) consider the noted
deficiencies and submit the
lacking documents to the
Audit Team. (8)

Management endeavor to fully

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) enjoin the (a)
Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE as well as the RPCLRN duly providing
all the necessary information, as discussed; (b) GSO to facilitate the renewal of LTO
registration of all motor vehicles, otherwise assess for repair or disposal, whichever is
deemed more advantageous; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the
impairment loss of unserviceable properties when its carrying costs are higher than
its recoverable costs; and (ii) recognize depreciation expense and include an
allowance for depreciation for all properties on the following month after its purchase
or completion.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (i) BAC and Accounting Office to
submit the lacking supporting documents, as discussed, and ensure the completeness
of documentation before the processing of payment of claim/s; (ii) Engineering Office
to enjoin the infrastructure project contractors to post the tarpaulin signboards with
sufficient details with the necessary update thereof of project status; and (iii)
Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate accounts in recording and reporting the
involved financial transactions.

We commended the MDRRM Council (MDRRMC) for substantially utilizing the
LDRRMF. However, we reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the (a)
MDRRMC to (i) carefully plan, implement, and prepare the LDRRMFIP so that it
contains all required information in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and list
of projects to be charged against the unexpended fund of prior years; and (ii) charge only
those PPAs which are directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response, and
rescue operations; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) disclose in the Notes to the
Financial Statements complete and necessary data for disaster risk reduction and
management transactions; and (ii) consider the noted deficiencies and submit the lacking
documents to the Audit Team.

Likewise, we recommended that the LCE order the concerned officials (i) to provide
suitable safeguard/s to the government property so that potential risk of loss could be
prevented; and (ii) to adhere to the imprest fund system principle by paying, through
check and in the name of the supplier, specifically for the goods acquired.

We recommended that the LCE enjoin the GFPS to (i) pursue the
implementation of well-planned targets in the GPB so that the gender issues indicated
therein could be fully addressed; (ii) observe the budget limits of expenditures per
planned activity; (iii) sex-disaggregate/quantify/specify the targets and results, as
necessary; (iv) assure that attribution is made using the HGDG tool and regard it in
the “attributed programs” section of the enhanced GPB and GAD AR template; (v)
constitute the GFPS and M&E Team in keeping with Items 4.1.A.2 and 5.2 of PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and (vi) submit the GPB and GAD AR within
the period set in COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MSWMB to (i) harmonize the
SWMP with the annual budgets of the Municipality; (ii) ascertain the regular review and
update of SWMP; (iii) intensify the SWMP’s implementation so that the scheduled
strategies for implementation for the year are fully achieved; and (iv) factor in the
establishment of monitoring and evaluation team.

We recommended that the LCE (i) enforce the authority of the Municipal
Treasurer to examine the books of businesses to ascertain the correctness of
assessment and collection of business tax; and (ii) instruct the MTO to submit from
business permit applicants the applicable documentary requirements and have it on
file to ensure compliance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the Municipal
Accountant to cause the reconciliation of accounts with the source agencies pertaining
to said dormant balances, liquidate and/or consider its write-off in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We further recommended that the Management enjoin the: (a) Project
Management Team for the Nego Kart project to: (i) strictly follow the stipulations in
the MOA; (ii) provide support and interventions to the Nego Kart beneficiaries to
boost sales; and (iii) impose guidelines to regulate the use of Nego Kart equipment;
and (b) Municipal Accountant to adjust the PPE and Government Equity accounts
by posting the proposed correcting entry.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal
Accountant to record the
corresponding adjustments to
the cash and liability accounts
for the total amount of checks
which are still outstanding but
have become stale, whether
unclaimed or while in the
hands of the payees, in order to
reflect the correct year-end
balances of cash and liability
accounts. (1)

We recommended that the
LCE direct the Municipal
Accountant to
(i) carefully review the
outstanding accounts payable
and select those which were
not covered by perfected
contract, against which no
actual claims have been filed,
whether administrative or
judicial, and had been
outstanding for more than two
years, and (2)
(ii) propose for the reversion of
said outstanding accounts
payable to the unappropriated
surplus of the GF. (3)

We recommended that the
LCE enjoin the Municipal
Accountant to immediately
reconcile his records with the
SAs and cause the refund of
the unused fund transfers, if
warranted. (13)

We recommended that the
Management instruct the
concerned officials to submit
the lacking documents stated
in the discussion. (14)

We recommended and the Property Officer and the Inventory Committee agreed to continue to exert best effort to locate the remaining missing properties and take immediate and appropriate action so that the persons accountable and responsible therefor should be held personally liable for the loss of the said properties.

3.15	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) order the (a) concerned officials to submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary supporting documents are attached to the DVs before certifying the payment.
3.16	The Management assured the Audit Team of their adherence to the audit recommendations.

4.27	We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct regular verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of receivables from other government agencies, special disbursing officers, and officers and employees, as well as the existence of payables to NGAs and LGUs so that the agency can (i) demand for immediate liquidation from implementing agencies of funds transferred in  their favor; (ii) request for write-off of non-moving accounts of 10 years or more; (iii) liquidate fund transfers received from other government agencies on time; and (iv) return to source agencies the unutilized funds from programs or projects that have long been completed.

5.7	We recommended that the LCE instruct the officials concerned to enforce the provisions in the loan agreement and pursue all legal remedies to collect the overdue accounts in order to protect the interest of the agency.

6.14	We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee continue to exert best effort to locate the remaining missing properties so that the persons accountable and responsible therefor could be held personally liable for the loss of the said properties.
6.15	We further recommended that the LGU fix the gaps in controlling the SEF assets by (i) agreeing with the concerned DepEd officials to defer the signing and releasing of the clearance from property accountability of DepEd personnel who were issued SEF assets until a separate clearance from property accountability is issued by the LGU in their favor and prior to their retirement, resignation, or transfer of office; and (ii)  contemplating on the transfer of movable and intended for use directly in the classroom SEF-funded properties to the DepEd, by way of donation, so that the person in actual physical possession/control of SEF property is made responsible for its proper use and safekeeping.
6.16	Finally, we recommended that the LCE direct the Accounting Office and other concerned department to exert best effort in gathering the necessary documents/records to facilitate the recognition in the books of accounts of the additional real properties as disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

7.5	We recommended that the LCE observe the requirements expressed in the said Circular by enjoining the Property Officer, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to maintain conformant pertinent reports and property records such that all of the local roads, together with their related components, are reported detailing the roads’ complete description and cost segregation.

8.21	We reiterated our recommendation that Management continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development fund so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented.
8.22	We also recommended that the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), together with the Municipal Budget Officer, prepare the AIP/Annual Budget clearly specifying the PPAs aimed at the attainment of socio-economic and environmental targets of the LGU; (b) BAC ensure that any amendment and/or adjustment in the procurement activities are fully reflected in the updated APP duly approved by the HoPE; and (c) Management ascertain that the delivery of public services, more specifically in the promotion of general welfare of the residents within its area of jurisdiction, are monitored so that the  funds are disbursed in line with its intended purpose.

9.20	We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the concerned Municipal Officers to (i) prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format; (ii) use the LDRRMF only for PPAs which are directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and (iii) submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team.
9.21	We further recommended that the LCE (a) together with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council carefully plan and implement PPAs under the LDRRM to attain utmost results and deliver the desired services to the residents of the Municipality; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to present completely in the Notes to the Financial Statements necessary data for disaster risk reduction and management transactions.

10.22	We recommended that the LCE direct the MSWMB to (i) include pre-identified solid wastes interventions in the annual budget of the agency to ensure funding for their implementation; (ii) monitor the implementation and completion of pre-identified solid waste interventions with the end in view of assuring that desired outputs and outcomes are fully achieved; (iii) organize monitoring and evaluation team and require them to prepare accomplishment report and progress reports in line with the SWMP; and (iv) review every two years or as the need arises, the SWMP for the purpose of ensuring its sustainability, viability and effectiveness.

11.22	We recommended that the LCE enjoin the GFPS to (i) monitor the GAD PPAs and coordinate with respective offices for the proper implementation of GAD Plan; (ii) enhance the GPB and Accomplishment Report by completely filling out the needed qualitative/quantitative aspects of the activities implemented including data on sex-disaggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary; (iii) use the HGDG tool to determine how much of the Agency’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD; (iv) carefully evaluate the PPAs which can be attributed to GAD to ensure that funds are utilized to address gender responsive issues; (v) fully comply with the provision of the JMC with respect to constitution of GFPS and M&E Team by including necessary representatives, as discussed, in accordance with Items 4.1.A.2 and 5.0 of the said law; and (vi) observe the deadlines in the submission of GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team.

12.12	We recommended that (a) the LCE instruct the (i) concerned official/s to carefully identify program or project that can be implemented in accordance with the guidelines issued by the national government relating to fund transfers; and (ii) Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payment. Submission of the lacking documents, as discussed, is also recommended; and (b) Management ascertain that the cost of Construction Safety and Health program is embedded in the project’s cost and ultimately in the contract or agreement between the LGU and the contractor.

11.22	We recommended that the LCE enjoin the GFPS to (i) monitor the GAD PPAs and coordinate with respective offices for the proper implementation of GAD Plan; (ii) enhance the GPB and Accomplishment Report by completely filling out the needed qualitative/quantitative aspects of the activities implemented including data on sex-disaggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary; (iii) use the HGDG tool to determine how much of the Agency’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD; (iv) carefully evaluate the PPAs which can be attributed to GAD to ensure that funds are utilized to address gender responsive issues; (v) fully comply with the provision of the JMC with respect to constitution of GFPS and M&E Team by including necessary representatives, as discussed, in accordance with Items 4.1.A.2 and 5.0 of the said law; and (vi) observe the deadlines in the submission of GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the (a) Management agreed to (i) stop the charging of inappropriate/improper expenditures to the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of the JMC; (ii) continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented; (b) BAC agreed to adhere with the GPPB Circular 2020-01 concerning the emergency procurement of goods and the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the conduct of public bidding; and (c) Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before effecting payment of contracted projects. We further recommended the submission of the lacking documents as discussed.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officers to (i) use the LDRRMF only for PPAs which are directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; (ii) prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format; (iii) submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 in the emergency procurement of goods.
observations.reason:
Verification made by the Audit Team revealed that there were still charges against the LDRRMF which did not align with the purpose thereof, as discussed in this report.

 The LDRRMFIP was still prepared non-conformant with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Thus, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MSWD officers and employees to (i) strictly follow the related provisions on the eligibility screening of identified recipients for government subsidy; (ii) recover or reproduce the missing SAC forms of the 86 ESP-SAP beneficiaries and submit to DSWD for their review for possible inclusion in future government subsidies;  (iii) inform the DSWD RO III of the name of the individual who returned the SAP 1st tranche for delisting; (iv) strictly comply with the applicable regulations on the documentation of ESP-SAP; and (v) submit the required lacking documents to support the propriety of receipt of the subsidy.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct all concerned agency officials and personnel to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and strictly observe the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to: (i) comply with the applicable guidelines in the accounting, recording and disbursement of cash donations and (ii) prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies to promote transparency and accountability in the administration of cash and in-kind donations.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to faithfully adhere to applicable tax laws and regulations in the withholding and remittance of taxes.
We recommended and the management agreed to continue to observe the applicable rules and regulations in the withholding and remittance of contributions and/or loan amortizations.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the Pag-IBIG premiums within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law.

We recommended and the Property Officer and the Inventory Committee agreed to continue to exert best effort to locate the remaining missing properties and take immediate and appropriate action so that the persons accountable and responsible therefor should be held personally liable for the loss of the said properties.

2.19 	We recommended and the (a) Management agreed to (i) stop the charging of inappropriate/improper expenditures to the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of the JMC; (ii) continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented; (b) BAC agreed to adhere with the GPPB Circular 2020-01 concerning the emergency procurement of goods and the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the conduct of public bidding; and (c) Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before effecting payment of contracted projects. We further recommended the submission of the lacking documents as discussed.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officers to (i) use the LDRRMF only for PPAs which are directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; (ii) prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format; (iii) submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 in the emergency procurement of goods.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MSWD officers and employees to (i) strictly follow the related provisions on the eligibility screening of identified recipients for government subsidy; (ii) recover or reproduce the missing SAC forms of the 86 ESP-SAP beneficiaries and submit to DSWD for their review for possible inclusion in future government subsidies;  (iii) inform the DSWD RO III of the name of the individual who returned the SAP 1st tranche for delisting; (iv) strictly comply with the applicable regulations on the documentation of ESP-SAP; and (v) submit the required lacking documents to support the propriety of receipt of the subsidy.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct all concerned agency officials and personnel to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and strictly observe the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to: (i) comply with the applicable guidelines in the accounting, recording and disbursement of cash donations and (ii) prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies to promote transparency and accountability in the administration of cash and in-kind donations.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to faithfully adhere to applicable tax laws and regulations in the withholding and remittance of taxes.
We recommended and the management agreed to continue to observe the applicable rules and regulations in the withholding and remittance of contributions and/or loan amortizations.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the (a) Property Officer (i) exert efforts to locate the missing properties; (ii) demand, in writing from the accountable officers to present the properties and/or to explain the loss of the same; and (iii) consistently issue AREs for each property issued; and (b) Local Chief Executive instruct the Investigating Committee to determine the circumstances surrounding the lost properties and impose sanction against accountable officers responsible.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) strongly seek the support and cooperation of the Sangguniang Bayan for the updating of the Economic Enterprises and Public Utilities Code and (b) instruct the Market Supervisor to (i) execute lease agreements with lessees presently occupying the stalls in the public market; and (ii) offer the vacant stalls to eligible applicants in order to help generate additional income for the agency.

We recommended and the (a) Management agreed to (i) stop the charging of inappropriate/improper expenditures to the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of the JMC; (ii) endeavor to implement the projects that were temporarily parked in the current year; and (iii) continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented; (b) Bids and Awards Committee agreed to (i) justify the awarding of the contract to the supplier of solar panels despite the deficiency stated above and (ii) strictly follow the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services; and (c) Municipal Accountant agreed to check the completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before effecting payment of contracted projects. We further recommended and the Management agreed to submit the lacking documents stated in the report.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to (i) prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format; and (ii) refrain from using the LDRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and (b)  Municipal Accountant coordinate with the BAC Secretariat for purposes of submitting the lacking supporting documents identified in this report.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the ME and the Bids and Awards Committee to (i) consider the timelines set for the project in the preparation and submission of documentary requirements prior to the conduct of procurement as provided in the relevant provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2019-71 to avoid incurrence of delays; (ii) furnish the Auditor advance copies of contracts of similar transactions and their supporting documents within five days from the execution thereof; (iii) ensure that similar projects are included in the Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality and (iv) submit the lacking documents supporting the payment for the construction project to avoid possible issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct: (a) the MENRO, in coordination with the SWMB, include pre-identified solid wastes interventions in the annual budget of the agency to ensure funding for their implementation, and (b) the MENRO and support offices to strictly implement the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 2007-81, specifically the proper segregation of wastes in order to instill discipline among constituents and attain the objectives R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the (a) Human Resources and Management Officer to i) comply with the relevant provisions of CSC Resolution No. 020790 in drafting the contracts of service/job order for all JO workers; and ii) ensure the correctness and completeness of details in the DTRs of JOs; and (b) Municipal Treasury and Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 specifically on the required documents before authorizing the automated payment for the casual employees’ salaries, the grant of cash advances for the payment of wages of JOs, and in the liquidation of the same.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and the management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the (a) Property Officer (i) exert efforts to locate the missing properties; (ii) demand, in writing from the accountable officers to present the properties and/or to explain the loss of the same; and (iii) consistently issue AREs for each property issued; and (b) Local Chief Executive instruct the Investigating Committee to determine the circumstances surrounding the lost properties and impose sanction against accountable officers responsible.

No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services; and (c) Municipal Accountant agreed to check the completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before effecting payment of contracted projects. We further recommended and the Management agreed to submit the lacking documents stated in the report.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct: (a) the MENRO, in coordination with the SWMB, include pre-identified solid wastes interventions in the annual budget of the agency to ensure funding for their implementation, and (b) the MENRO and support offices to strictly implement the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 2007-81, specifically the proper segregation of wastes in order to instill discipline among constituents and attain the objectives R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the (a) Human Resources and Management Officer to i) comply with the relevant provisions of CSC Resolution No. 020790 in drafting the contracts of service/job order for all JO workers; and ii) ensure the correctness and completeness of details in the DTRs of JOs; and (b) Municipal Treasury and Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 specifically on the required documents before authorizing the automated payment for the casual employees’ salaries, the grant of cash advances for the payment of wages of JOs, and in the liquidation of the same.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the Pag-IBIG premiums within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law.
We recommended and the management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require all Collecting Officers to turn over their collections intact daily at the close of business day to the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer to preclude the misuse and/or misappropriation of cash in their possession.

We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to closely monitor the issuance of Accountable Forms to the Collecting Officers, instruct them to issue ORs in strict numerical sequence and to accomplish properly the AFs being issued with complete details and countersign to prove unintentional errors and stop allowing non-bonded employee to collect revenue.

Furthermore, we recommended and the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer agreed to verify and count, not only the cash remitted but also the unissued accountable forms presented and ensure that all collections are remitted intact by all collectors concerned to prevent loss or misuse of government funds.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Property Officer agreed to (a) continue exert efforts to locate the remaining missing properties; (b) demand, in writing from the accountable officers to present the properties and/or to explain the loss of the same; and (c) renew all expired AREs for each property issued. 

We further reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed a) to create a committee to investigate the circumstances surrounding the lost properties and impose sanction against accountable officers responsible therefor; and (b) authorize the designated Property Officer to attend trainings offered by the accredited agencies on supply and property management to be aware of the rules and procedures in managing, safeguarding and disposal of PPE.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the BAC and its Secretariat, the Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to exercise due diligence in preparing documents and processing disbursements and to ensure that required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers. 

Furthermore, we reiterated that the deficiencies noted in Annex B be submitted or settled immediately.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat Office and the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents and to observe the instructions on contract submission prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-01; and the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Human Resource and Management Office to ensure compliance on setting the minimum limit of monthly take home pay of Municipal employees in accordance with prevailing statute.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) intensify further the conduct of Information and Education Campaigns to educate and make the residents aware of the existing environmental laws and policies with their corresponding fines and penalties; (b)  instruct the designated enforcers to ensure that the corresponding fines and penalties will be served to violators to reduce the instances of prohibited acts such as littering, open burning, and collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste; and (c) instruct the solid waste management board to continue to monitor the effective implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal GFPS agreed to a) continue to strengthen its efforts in the accomplishment of all the specified GAD Activities to be able to attain their corresponding GAD objectives; and b) continue to update and maintain the existing GAD Database for GAD decision making purposes.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents; and

Management strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR on the procurement and documentation process and the DILG Memorandum Circular on the implementation of the project.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to require the Municipal LSB to refrain from accommodating expenditures that are not explicitly provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments and recognize financial transactions properly to avoid misstatement of affected accounts.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Property Officer (a) continue exert efforts to locate the remaining missing properties; (b) demand, in writing from the accountable officers to present the properties and/or to explain the loss of the same; and (c) renew all expired AREs for each property issued. We further reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive a) create a committee to investigate the circumstances surrounding the lost properties and impose sanction against accountable officers responsible therefor; and (b) authorize the designated Property Officer to attend trainings offered by the accredited agencies on supply and property management to be aware of the rules and procedures in managing, safeguarding and disposal of PPE.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the accounting office the necessary documents and reports pertaining to the demolished structure so that the “Markets” account may be adjusted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC and its Secretariat, the Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to exercise utmost diligence in processing disbursements and handling other transactions to ensure that required supporting documents are properly attached and/or accomplished.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) intensify further the conduct of Information and Education Campaigns to make the residents aware of the existing environmental laws and policies with their corresponding fines and penalties; (b) instruct the designated enforcers to ensure that the corresponding fines and penalties will be served to violators to reduce the instances of prohibited acts such as littering, opening burning, and collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste; and (c) require the solid waste management board to continue to monitor the effective implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal GFPS: (a) enhance its capacity especially in setting attainable targets/measures to be able to formulate a better GAD Plan; (b) continue to strengthen its efforts in the accomplishment of all the specified GAD Activities to be able to attain their corresponding GAD Objectives; and (c) continue to update and maintain the existing GAD Database in every possible occasion.

We recommended that Management: (a) consider the timelines of project implementation and improve its monitoring functions or supervision for projects being implemented to avoid the incurrence of delays; and (b) direct the Bids and Awards Committee to i) strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities; and ii) provide justification on the deficiencies noted and immediately submit the lacking documents enumerated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and Advisory No. 2016-02 dated November 25, 2016 of the Interim National Peace and Order Council Secretariat of DILG relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, and use of the confidential funds.

We recommended that Management: (i) ensure the timely and effective implementation of the formulated PPAs; and (ii) consider outsourcing or partnership with private organizations and entities that could provide sponsorship or financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC PPAs.
observations.reason: The budget for some 2018 LCPC PPAs were not utilized because funds were received from other government agencies. On-going talks with different organizations to be able to establish partnership/sponsorship.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LSB to: (a) follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and (b) refrain from accommodating expenditures that are explicitly provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to reconcile the withheld amounts and actual remittance periodically to ensure that accurate trust obligations are remitted in full.

We recommended that the
Municipality (a)
expedite the
development of a GAD
Database to be able to
use the collected data
in making more relevant
decisions; (b) conduct
more capability-building
and enhancement
trainings, especially in
the administration of the
HGDG to be able to
better identify and
prioritize GAD projects
and programs; (c) make
certain that the GAD
Plan and Budget is
verified by the PPDO to
ensure that the PPAs of
the Municipality
support that of the
Province; (d) ensure
that every identified
gender issue, its cause,
and the objectives set to
address it are
harmonized and in line
with each other to be
able to formulate a
more responsive GAD
Plan and Budget; (e)
implement and
accomplish all the
activities specified in its
GAD Plan and Budget to
realize all the set
objectives; and (f)
continue its efforts in
gender mainstreaming to
be able to
institutionalize GAD
within the whole
community for the
benefit of not only a
particular gender or
sector, but for the
benefit and equality of
all.

We recommend that the
Local School Board
(LSB) strictly comply
with the provisions of
R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No.
7160, LOI 1462 and
relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG
Joint Circulars in the
preparation of the SEF
Annual Budget and
identify the priority
expenditures pursuant	hereto treating on the
utilization of the Special
Education Fund to avoid
disallowances in audit.

We recommended that
management direct the
accountable officers to
immediately settle
their cash advances in
order to comply with
the provisions of COA
Circular 97-002 and to
record all expenses in
the proper period.

We reiterated our
recommendation that the
Municipal Mayor
instruct – (a) the
Treasury Office to
prepare and furnish the
Accounting Office the
list required under
Section 20 Volume I of
the Manual on the NGAS
for LGUs; and (b) the
Municipal Accountant to
base the establishment
of RPT and SET
receivables on the list
submitted by the
Treasury Office for an
accurate recognition of
the said receivables.

We reiterated our
recommendations that
the Property Officer
(a) exert efforts to
locate the missing
properties; (b) demand,
in writing, the
determined accountable
officers to present the
properties and/or to

same; and (c) prepare
ARE for each property
issued. We further

Local Chief Executive
(a) designate a full time
Property Officer who
will maintain, update
and keep records of the
property, plant and
equipment of the
Municipality including
safeguarding the proper
use and application of
the assets and
monitoring their
whereabouts and

Officer to attend

on supply and property
management to be aware

We recommended that the
Local  Chief  Executive
(a) instruct DepEd
employees to turn-over
to the Municipality the
issued properties upon
transfer to other
school or town; and b)
request the DepEd to
inform the Municipality
for every movement of
DepEd personnel
assigned in Bamban for
the Municipal Treasurer
to be able to monitor
the proper turn-over of
issued properties to
the employees who will
be moving out from a
certain school.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
require the MDRRMO to
(a) refrain from using
MDRRMF for

and rescue operations; and (b) prepare the

LDRRMF.

Municipal DRRM
Council, cause the

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive:
(a) recommend to the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB)
to continuously work on
creating policies, such
as “No-littering”
ordinance; (b)
intensify its
Information and
Education Campaigns
(IECs) to educate and
make the residents
aware of existing
policies regarding
waste management for
their own benefit; (c)
encourage every
barangay officials to
actively and
continuously participate
in implementing the
provisions of R.A. No.
9003 specially the
segregation of waste at
source; and (d)
prioritize the collection
and disposal of waste
by the garbage trucks
instead of the delivery
of sand to barangays.

separate vehicle for the

to cause delay in the

sanitary landfill.

Local Chief Executive

previous recommendation

2016.

concerned LGU officials
comply with the
provision of COA
Circular No. 2009-001 to
avoid suspension of the
transactions and the

law under 4.1 of this
Circular.

management (a) stop the
practice of procuring
goods and services thru
reimbursement unless

1445 and the guidelines

to enable the proper

No. 9184 on the
conditions before the
Municipality can adopt

procurement as
alternative modes of
procurement.

previous recommendation
that management comply
with the minimum limit
of P3,500.00 for
employees’ monthly net
take home pay pursuant
to Section 52 of GAA
2016.

We recommended that management direct the accountable officers to immediately settle their cash advances in order to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and to record all expenses in the proper period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct – (a) the Treasury Office to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Property Officer (a) exert efforts to locate the missing properties; (b) demand, in writing, the determined accountable officers to present the properties and/or to explain the loss of the same; and (c) prepare ARE for each property issued. We further reiterated that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to investigate the circumstances surrounding the lost properties and impose sanction against accountable officers responsible therefor.
We also recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive (a) designate  a full time  Property Officer who will maintain, update and  keep records of the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality including safeguarding the  proper use and application of the assets and monitoring their whereabouts and existence; and (b) authorize the designated Property Officer to attend trainings offered by the Commission on Audit and the Department of Budget and Management on supply and property management to be aware of the rules and procedures in managing, safeguarding and disposal of PPE and be conscious  of  his duties and responsibilities including the accountability attached to such designation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) instruct DepEd employees to turn-over to the Municipality the issued properties upon transfer to other school or town; and b) request the DepEd to inform the Municipality for every movement of DepEd personnel assigned in Gerona for the Municipal Treasurer to be able to monitor the proper turn-over of issued properties to the employees who will be moving out from a certain school.

We recommend that the management:
submit documents relative to Roads 3 and 4 to conduct complete technical evaluation;
immediately institute available remedial measures to justify and rectify the deficiencies and lapses detailed above; and
instruct the Engineering Office to cautiously comply with the standards of technical document preparation, and ensure completeness of technical documents for audit, inspection and evaluation purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC and its Secretariat, the Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents for us to be able to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that the Municipality (a) expedite the development of a GAD Database to be able to use the collected data in making more relevant decisions; (b) conduct more capability-building and enhancement trainings, especially in the administration of the HGDG to be able to better identify and prioritize GAD projects and programs; (c) make certain that the GAD Plan and Budget is verified by the PPDO to ensure that the PPAs of the Municipality support that of the Province; (d) ensure that every identified gender issue, its cause, and the objectives set to address it are harmonized and in line with each other to be able to formulate a more responsive GAD Plan and Budget; (e) implement and accomplish all the activities specified in its GAD Plan and Budget to realize all the set objectives; and (f) continue its efforts in gender mainstreaming to be able to institutionalize GAD within the whole community for the benefit of not only a particular gender or sector, but for the benefit and equality of all.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRMO to (a) refrain from using MDRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and (b) prepare the LDRRMFIP before the release and use of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as chairman of the Municipal DRRM Council, cause the selection and accreditation of CSOs and private sector representative in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned memorandum circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to continuously work on creating policies, such as “No-littering” ordinance; (b) intensify its Information and Education Campaigns (IECs) to educate and make the residents aware of existing policies regarding waste management for their own benefit; (c) encourage every barangay officials to actively and continuously participate in implementing the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specially the segregation of waste at source; and (d) prioritize the collection and disposal of waste by the garbage trucks instead of the delivery of sand to barangays. Better yet, provide a separate vehicle for the delivery of sand so not to cause delay in the hauling of waste to the sanitary landfill.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No. 7160, LOI 1462 and relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto treating on the utilization of the Special Education Fund to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid suspension of the transactions and the penalty prescribed by law under 4.1 of this Circular.

We recommended that management (a) stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursement unless the item/service is petty in nature; (b) comply strictly with Section 93 of PD No. 1445 and the guidelines and principles of an imprest fund wherein regular, recurring and non-petty expenses should all be paid in check in the name of the supplier for internal control purposes; and (c) support disbursements with complete required supporting documents, to enable the proper evaluation of the transactions.  We recommended further that management comply with the provisions of Sections 52 and 53 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the conditions before the Municipality can adopt shopping and negotiated procurement as alternative modes of procurement.
We reiterated our previous recommendation that management comply with the minimum limit of P3,500.00 for employees’ monthly net take home pay pursuant to Section 52 of GAA 2016.
We recommended that GSIS premiums deducted be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law and be properly monitored to avoid over-remittance.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Revenue Regulation No. 2-28 dated April 7, 1998, as amended by Revenue Regulation Nos. 14-2002 and 10-2008 dated September 9, 2002 and July 8, 2008, respectively, in the withholding and remittance of taxes to avoid the misuse of the same.
